project_title: Sarcasm Detection in Social Media Text using RoBERTa
input_strategy: Text-only input using parent_comment + comment when context is available
main_risk_statement: The system is a research and coursework demonstration. It should not be used to automatically remove, flag, penalize, or judge user content.

risk_register.reason: Sarcasm depends on context, tone, culture, and speaker intent. A wrong prediction may label genuine text as sarcastic or miss subtle sarcasm.
risk_register.mitigation: Show confidence score, communicate uncertainty, avoid automated punitive use, and require human interpretation.

risk_register.reason: The model only uses text input and may not have full conversation history, user relationship, timing, or social context.
risk_register.mitigation: Use optional parent comment context, state that predictions are approximate, and avoid using the model for moderation decisions.

risk_register.reason: The SARC dataset comes from Reddit and may reflect platform-specific language, communities, humor styles, and demographic patterns.
risk_register.mitigation: Limit claims to Reddit-style/social-media text and disclose that performance may not generalize to other platforms or cultures.

risk_register.reason: Social media text may contain personal or sensitive information even if collected publicly.
risk_register.mitigation: Do not commit raw error samples to GitHub, keep local examples ignored, and use aggregate reporting instead of exposing raw user text.

risk_register.reason: A high probability score does not mean the prediction is always correct, especially for ambiguous sarcasm.
risk_register.mitigation: Present confidence as model confidence only, not truth certainty. Include clear wording in the UI.

risk_register.reason: A hosted app could be misunderstood as a production-ready moderation tool.
risk_register.mitigation: Add a visible research-demo warning and disable heavy training/execution features on public hosting.

risk_register.reason: Training used a practical sample size due to local compute constraints, while evaluation used the full held-out test set.
risk_register.mitigation: Report the training setup transparently and keep scripts/configs reproducible in GitHub.

risk_register.reason: Sarcasm may vary across cultures, languages, dialects, memes, and community norms.
risk_register.mitigation: Avoid claiming universal sarcasm detection and recommend further testing on other communities or languages.

responsible_use_rules: Use the model for academic demonstration and exploratory analysis only.
Do not use the model to automatically remove, flag, penalize, or judge users.
Show the prediction confidence and communicate uncertainty.
Avoid exposing raw Reddit text samples in public reports or repositories.
Keep human review in the loop for any interpretation of model outputs.
Disclose dataset, preprocessing, evaluation, and model limitations clearly.
